label: Reparameterization & differentiable sampling

node.label: Reparameterization & differentiable sampling
node.definition: Random sampling is reformulated so gradients can pass through stochastic latent variables.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Reparameterization & differentiable sampling is modeled as depending on Probability theory, Latent variables & hidden state and feeding downstream nodes such as Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields, Variational autoencoders, Diffusion & score-based models.
examples: atlas downstream link to Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields
atlas downstream link to Variational autoencoders
atlas downstream link to Diffusion & score-based models
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Latent variables & hidden state.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Reparameterization & differentiable sampling has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Reparameterization & differentiable sampling has a `uses` dependency on Latent variables & hidden state because Latent variables & hidden state is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Reparameterization & differentiable sampling has a `uses` dependency on Calculus & automatic differentiation theory because Calculus & automatic differentiation theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
